justification: The diff correctly updates Spring Boot to 3.3.13 and Java to 21, and performs necessary javax -> jakarta namespace migrations (e.g., ArticlesController.java, UserDto.java). It also updates deprecated Spring Security configuration (authorizeRequests -> authorizeHttpRequests) and fixes deprecated MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON_UTF8. However, it introduces unnecessary churn by removing unused imports (java.util.Optional, org.antlr.v4.runtime.misc.NotNull) and adding redundant dependencies (jakarta.servlet-api, jakarta.persistence-api) that are likely already managed by Spring Boot starters. The removal of the junit 4 dependency is good, but the diff lacks Java 21-specific idioms (e.g., virtual threads, pattern matching for switch) and includes some spurious changes.
